data.title: Will the US government shut down again and the cryptocurrency market be hit hard?
data.description:
Last October, the US government shutdown lasted 43 days, causing a global financial liquidity crunch and a sharp drop in the cryptocurrency market. Many people still vividly remember that event. And at the end of this month, something similar may happen again. Three days ago, Trump said in an interview in Davos, "I think we're in trouble again, and it's very likely we'll be facing another government shutdown caused by the Democrats." Although lawmakers are working hard to finalize an appropriations agreement, with the January 30th deadline fast approaching, the US government only has four working days left, making another shutdown seem difficult to avoid. Currently, the probability of the US government shutting down again before January 31st on Polymarket has surged to 80%. The main points of contention between the two parties are funding for ICE and Obamacare. These are also long-standing issues in the political and economic debates between the two parties: immigration policy and social welfare. To further understand why a government shutdown might occur, we must begin with one of the largest welfare fraud cases in U.S. history, which took place in Minnesota. It all started in Minnesota. The story begins in 2020 when the pandemic had just begun. The United States has a traditional welfare policy: providing free lunches to children from impoverished families. Before the pandemic, this welfare was strictly regulated; children had to eat together at schools or formal community centers, and roll call was required to prevent fraudulent claims. But with the pandemic, schools closed, and children stayed home. So the US Congress made a bold move, allowing meals to be taken away without strict verification. As long as you are a registered non-profit organization, the government will provide funding for whatever meals you claim to have distributed, with no upper limit. This loophole is the background to the Minnesota welfare fraud case, exposed by an American social media blogger, Nick Shirley. In December 2025, Nick Shirley released a 42-minute investigative video that went viral overnight. In the video, he exposed a group of non-profit organizations operating under the guise of "children's nutrition" and "assistance to vulnerable groups." These organizations applied for funding from state and federal governments, claiming to serve tens of thousands of people, but in reality, many of these children did not exist, nor did the children's meals. Their so-called charitable projects were merely shells used to obtain government funding. The video quickly went viral, garnering tens of millions of views within the first 24 hours. With various short video edits and reposts, the overall reach exceeded one hundred million times. Following investigations by the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) and the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), it was discovered that since 2018, the federal government had allocated a total of $18 billion to 14 public programs in Minnesota, with $9 billion of that amount involved in the fraud. This is one of the largest welfare fraud cases in U.S. history. The truly politically explosive aspect of this case lies in the fact that it occurred in Minnesota. Minnesota has long been a stronghold for the Democratic Party, and its Democratic governor was Kamala Harris's running mate. It is also a state highly dependent on welfare programs and with an exceptionally high concentration of non-profit organizations. Over the past decade, its welfare system has developed a structure of "outsourced governance": the government does not directly provide services but instead delegates many public functions to non-profit organizations. Theoretically, this is for efficiency and community autonomy; however, in reality, it has created an extremely lax, poorly regulated, and politically complex gray area. Many of the organizations involved have close ties to the local Democratic Party political landscape. Evidence suggests that a significant portion of the funds obtained by these welfare fraud organizations flowed into Democratic campaign donations. Meanwhile, Minnesota itself is a highly immigrant state, with a large Somali and other immigrant population. The Minnesota Attorney General's Office stated that 82 of the 92 defendants in this case are Somali Americans. This highly intertwines immigration enforcement, welfare distribution, and public safety issues, precisely hitting the core of the long-standing conflict between the Democratic and Republican parties, and also key policy promises repeatedly emphasized by Trump and the Republican Party during their campaigns. Since someone has handed them a knife, the Republican Party has naturally chosen to plunge it in hard. Both Donald Trump and Elon Musk, the biggest "internet celebrities" of the current administration, frequently retweeted related content, fiercely criticizing Minnesota's handling of the situation and linking such opaque and potentially abused subsidy policies to the Democratic Party's long-standing expansion of social welfare. Due to the exposure of the Minnesota welfare fraud case, Trump significantly intensified immigration enforcement in Minnesota. The Department of Homeland Security and the FBI deployed a large number of agents to continue the investigation and crackdown on illegal immigrants, with ICE (Immigration and Customs Enforcement), as the enforcement agency under the Department of Homeland Security, becoming the main force in this operation. However, the sudden increase in enforcement quickly led to serious consequences. On January 7, ICE agents accidentally shot and killed a 37-year-old woman, Renée Good, during an operation, drawing national attention. Just 17 days later, on January 24, another U.S. citizen, Alex Pretti, was accidentally shot and killed by federal immigration enforcement officers in the same city. These two fatal shootings caused the situation in Minnesota to spiral out of control. Large-scale protests and riots erupted, even requiring the deployment of the National Guard to maintain order. The Democratic Party quickly seized this opportunity, using the fatal shootings by ICE in Minnesota as irrefutable evidence of the agency's out-of-control enforcement methods.

People spontaneously mourn the victims shot and killed by law enforcement officers

So why did this affect the US government shutdown on January 31?

In the US constitutional system, the purse strings are held by Congress, and the executive branch cannot decide on its own to continue spending money.

Each fiscal year, Congress must pass 12 annual appropriations bills, each corresponding to one of 12 policy areas: defense, homeland security, agriculture, transportation, housing, etc. These appropriations bills determine the maximum amount of money a department can spend in that fiscal year, and where that money can be spent. If an appropriations proposal fails to pass, or if the legal authorization for the fiscal year expires and Congress hasn't passed a new authorization in time, the department will be without a budget and will have to shut down. This is what's known as a government shutdown. The normal process is that the fiscal year begins on October 1st. If no agreement is reached before October 1st, Congress will pass a temporary appropriations bill to extend the government's life, setting a new deadline. January 30th, which we are currently focusing on, is the expiration date of this temporary bill. If, by that day, a formal appropriations bill has not been passed, and the temporary bill has not been renewed, the US government will be shut down, or partially shut down. These appropriations bills require passage by both the House of Representatives and the Senate. The House has already signed them, but the process is stalled in the Senate. The US Senate stipulates that a government appropriations bill requires 60 votes to pass. Currently, the Senate's seat structure is: 53 Republicans, 45 Democrats, plus two independent senators allied with the Democrats, giving the Democrats a total of 47 votes. Even if all Republicans agree, they only have 53 votes, insufficient to unilaterally reach the 60 required to end the debate. This means that if the Democrats collectively obstruct the process, the Republicans must secure at least 7 votes from the Democratic camp to bring the appropriations bill to a final vote and prevent a government shutdown. This is why Trump has been proposing to abolish the 60-vote threshold for the past six months.

Therefore, in this context, the Department of Homeland Security budget, including ICE, has become the most controversial and difficult part to reach a consensus on in the current funding negotiations involving the risk of a government shutdown.

There are many voices on social media supporting ICE law enforcement.

The Democrats' logic is clear: ICE caused two deaths in Minnesota, which proves that the agency's law enforcement methods have serious problems.

Why should we continue to fund ICE without substantial reforms and stricter restrictions? Democrats are demanding cuts to ICE or at least stricter limitations. Republicans counter with the same argument: the Minnesota welfare fraud case, involving $9 billion and mostly Somali defendants, underscores the need to strengthen, not weaken, immigration enforcement. ICE is a key force in combating illegal immigration and welfare fraud and must be adequately funded. This antagonism has led to a congressional deadlock on the Department of Homeland Security budget bill, which includes funding for ICE. This issue may even remain a partisan battleground until the midterm elections, becoming a central battleground. Beyond ICE funding, the issue of healthcare subsidies represents the second, and more structural, point of contention in the current government shutdown risk. This controversy stems from a legacy issue temporarily shelved during the previous government shutdown and remains unresolved: whether to continue increasing funding for the Affordable Care Act (ACA), commonly known as Obamacare. These subsidies were initially temporary measures introduced during the COVID-19 pandemic, significantly reducing the actual cost of health insurance for low- and middle-income individuals through tax credits. They were not made permanent after the pandemic but officially expired at the end of last year. Due to the failure of Democrats and Republicans to reach an agreement on funding authorization, this issue was "frozen" during the previous government shutdown, but it hasn't disappeared; it has simply been delayed until now. Democrats want increased funding; if the subsidies are not renewed, the health insurance premiums for millions of Americans will surge in the short term, and some may even be forced to withdraw from the insurance system altogether. However, the Republican opposition stems from similar backgrounds and causes to the Minnesota welfare fraud case. The healthcare subsidy system during the pandemic has bred systemic fraud. The ACA subsidy is not merely a financial burden issue, but a "gray pool" abused by local nonprofits, insurance companies, and even political networks. Politics influences people's livelihoods, and people's livelihoods also influence politics. The period of contention between the two parties over this healthcare budget is intricately linked to several hotly debated events online. For example, the "American cutoff line" theory, which recently sparked heated discussion in Chinese-speaking regions, states that many American families are not destitute; they have jobs, income, and health insurance, but their financial security margin is extremely low. Once they face unemployment, serious illness, accidental injury, or the expiration of health insurance subsidies and increased premiums, their cash flow can be completely depleted in a very short time, plunging them into a state of inescapable financial ruin. Mortgage defaults, credit card defaults, and snowballing medical bills occur almost simultaneously. Like a character in a game, once their health drops to a critical threshold, a single critical hit is enough to eliminate them from the game, without needing a combo. The ACA subsidy serves as the last buffer for many families to avoid triggering this "kill line." It doesn't make people rich, but it prevents them from falling out of the system entirely after an illness or layoff. This is why the Democratic Party describes the subsidy issue as an "affordability crisis" rather than "welfare expansion." It is against this social backdrop that the case that ignited public opinion—a 26-year-old third-generation rich Ivy League graduate who shot and killed the CEO of America's largest insurance company—why did it fulfill the American public's imagination of a modern-day "folk hero"?

The suspect in the shooting of the CEO, Luigi

That symbolized insurance company CEO became a victim. Healthcare issues are no longer just policy debates, but are eroding the very foundation of social security.

When people begin to use extreme events to express their despair for a system, it indicates that the space for discussion about that system has become severely unbalanced.

When people begin to use extreme events to express their despair for a system, it indicates that the space for discussion about that system has become severely unbalanced.

... The ACA subsidy dispute, occurring precisely within this imbalance, has been pushed to the intersection of Congress, the election, and a government shutdown. Will this shutdown cause another crash in the cryptocurrency market? Will the impact of this US government shutdown cause a cryptocurrency market crash like the last one? I think there will still be a negative impact, but the degree may not be as high as last time. The main reason is that Congress has already passed 6 out of 12 annual appropriations bills. This means that if an overall agreement is not reached by the end of January, the shutdown will be a "partial shutdown," not a full shutdown. This is a fundamental difference compared to the shutdown in October 2025. The last shutdown was due to a complete budget system failure, lasting 43 days and setting a historical record. This time, even if it happens again, it will primarily target the Department of Homeland Security and a few departments that have not yet received funding. Currently, the cryptocurrency market seems to have anticipated this, with prices falling in advance. Related reading: "Why Bitcoin Keeps Falling" (https://www.theblockbeats.info/news/61045). Furthermore, the impact of this government shutdown on the crypto industry may also be reflected at the institutional level. If the budget impasse continues, Congress will be forced to focus all its political energy on the lowest priority goal of "avoiding a complete shutdown," and other issues—especially bills requiring bipartisan coordination and with complex technical details—will be systematically shelved. The most critical of these is the Clarity Act, which the crypto industry is highly concerned about. The significance of this bill lies not in short-term stimulus, but in institutional certainty: clarifying whether digital assets are securities or commodities, defining the regulatory boundaries between the SEC and CFTC, and providing a compliance anchor for exchanges, DeFi projects, and institutional capital. The bill passed the House of Representatives in July and was originally expected to enter the Senate for consideration in January. However, if the government shuts down again, this timeline is likely to be postponed again. This will not immediately depress cryptocurrency prices, but it will slow the pace of institutional investment and weaken the certainty of the medium- to long-term narrative. In summary, even if the US government shuts down again in January, its direct impact on the financial markets, especially cryptocurrency prices, is unlikely to replicate the magnitude of the previous shutdown. The current shutdown risk has been highly anticipated and is more limited in scale. However, we can see more of a "prelude" to the midterm elections at the end of the year in this US government shutdown. Whether it's ICE funding, ACA Medicaid subsidies, or the tug-of-war surrounding welfare fraud and healthcare affordability, these controversies are highly relevant to voters' daily lives and can easily be transformed into clear, conflicting, and easily disseminated political narratives. The government shutdown is evolving from a budget failure into a political battleground being prepared by both sides for the midterm elections, setting the tone for the political and policy direction in the coming months.

data.title: Pantera Partner: The Current State and Future of the Internet Capital Market
data.description:
Original Article: "Internet Capital Markets - From the Trenches to Wall Street"

Compiled by: Ken, Chaincatcher

"Internet Capital Markets" (ICM) has been one of the hottest buzzwords of the past year. But what exactly does it mean? People often lump everything together into this broad concept—from Pumpfun to Sonar to xStocks, and sometimes even stablecoin payments, new types of banks, and lending protocols! But this confuses fundamentally different financial instruments with different value capture logics and ownership structures.

In my view, "Internet Capital Markets" is simply another way of saying "fundraising using tokens"—whether the fundraising target is Memecoin or tokenized stocks.

I suggest classifying ICM asset classes according to their "lifecycle" based on the following points: How mature are the tokenized projects? What kind of asset is the fundraising activity endorsing (attention, reputation, product, revenue, or governance)? How does value flow and accumulate between the platform and the asset itself? 1 - The Lifecycle of the ICM Platform The appeal of public blockchains lies in the fact that anyone can issue tokens, regardless of the project's maturity. Therefore, various issuance and tokenization platforms have emerged to meet the needs of projects at different stages. I believe ICM projects can generally be divided into the following five maturity stages:

Stage 1 - Anonymous Stage

  • Representative Platforms: Pumpfun, Bonk Launchpad

  • Representative Assets: Fartcoin, WIF, MOODENG

In this stage, most tokens issued are pure Meme coins.

Tokens issued on Pumpfun or Bonk typically lack any strong "fundamentals" or "team" backing. Token pricing at this stage is entirely driven by zeitgeist, financial engineering, and the "animal spirits" of the attention market.

Second Stage - Creator Stage

  • Representative Platforms: Zora, Believe App

  • Representative Assets: JESSE, BALAJI, PASTERNAK

In the creator stage, there is usually a well-known creator or team behind them. The token can be seen as a financialized form of the token creator's (or claimed creator's) "attention" or "reputation." For example, on Zora, the top two tokens, JESSE and BALAJI, are associated with specific real-world figures.

Third Stage - Venture Capital Stage

  • Representative Platforms: MetaDAO, StreetApp

  • Representative Assets: Avici, Umbra, Kled

At the venture capital stage, these tokens no longer resemble "meme coins" but are closer to traditional venture capital fundraising mechanisms.

Projects on MetaDAO and Street (such as Avici, Umbra, and Kled) are similar in quality to projects typically backed by blue-chip VCs in their seed to Series A rounds. They have established teams, roadmaps, viable product proposals, and potential governance rights. The real tension and issue here gradually evolves into how to balance the relationship between equity holders (and traditional VCs) and token issuance—while token issuance expands capital access, it cannot provide equivalent legal protection and governance oversight. Phase Four - The "ICO" Phase

  • Representative Platforms: Binance, Upbit, Sonar

  • Representative Assets: MegaETH, Plasma, Octra

The fourth phase is the "ICO" phase. At this stage, projects typically have significant market traction, have secured venture capital funding, and may have a stable revenue stream. Similar to an "IPO," token issuance at this stage represents the launch of a more mature and stable product that has demonstrated some market performance. Centralized exchanges such as Binance, Coinbase, and Upbit, as well as professional ICO platforms like Sonar, typically operate at this stage.

In this stage, the tension between tokens and equity becomes more pronounced—existing venture capitalists often acquire token rights, but these tokens may only grant governance rights to specific revenue streams, IP, or contracts, rather than equity. This leads to a fragmentation of asset ownership, and tokens are often treated as "second-class citizens" compared to equity. The fifth stage - Public Asset Stage. Representative platforms: xStocks, NYSE Tokenized Securities. Representative assets: xNVIDIA, BENJI, PAXG. The final stage is the public asset stage, which involves the tokenization of existing financial instruments. Discussions about RWA are numerous and will not be elaborated upon here. The core logic is that "tokenized" assets (such as xNVIDIA, BENJI, and PAXG) are "mirrors" of existing "real" financial instruments. Tokenized versions broaden the accessibility of on-chain audiences, while "real" assets retain all governance and legal rights. 2 - Where Does Value Go in the Internet Capital Market? After analyzing platforms and assets at various "stages" of the internet capital market, a natural question arises—where does value actually accumulate? My working hypothesis is that this actually depends on the stage of the market—the earlier the stage, the more value tends to belong to the platform; the later the stage, the more value tends to belong to the asset. In the early stages (stages one and two), I believe value will flow to the platform itself—such as Pumpfun or Zora. This is because tokenized assets themselves have no fundamental support, and their viral spread has a certain degree of randomness. The rules of the game in the early stages lie in the frequency and massive scale of project issuance on the platform. Pumpfun has generated over $800 million in revenue through its transaction fees and "graduation fees," thanks to the sheer number of tokens issued on its platform. However, in the later stages of the internet capital market lifecycle (Phase III and beyond), I believe holding the actual assets themselves (such as Avici, Kled, MegaETH, xNVIDIA) may capture more value than holding the underlying tokenized platform. In these later stages, projects typically have strong fundamentals, a clear roadmap, product, and potential monetization paths, and holding the assets (rather than platform tokens) allows them to capture these upside gains. In this context, the issuing platform is merely an access point, and often not the only way to gain exposure to the asset during the "ICO" and "public asset" phases. Furthermore, the stronger the asset's fundamentals, the stronger the bargaining power over the issuing platform. For example, Kled, a groundbreaking crowdsourced AI data marketplace project, initially launched on Believe but migrated to Street after token controversies, illustrates this point. However, centralized exchanges (CEXs) are an exception to this rule, and they are typically highly profitable businesses. However, for major CEXs like Binance and Coinbase, their competitive advantage lies not only in listing tokens, but more importantly in the liquidity and trading volume of specific assets. In other words, their moat lies in having a large potential user base to trade your tokens (and thus earn fees), not just the listing process itself. 3 - What's Next for ICM Projects? Over the past year, I believe innovation in the ICM space has generally exhibited a "dumbbell" structure. On one end are Launchpads like Pumpfun industrially mass-producing tokens and driving on-chain trading volume on chains like Solana; on the other end are banks and other traditional financial institutions tokenizing traditional assets (from money market instruments to stocks to commodities) to leverage access to global liquidity. However, in my view, a more interesting observation lies in how these two phenomena converge in the middle of the ICM lifecycle—namely, the third and fourth stages (venture capital and ICO stages). Currently, through mechanisms such as Street's ERC-S mechanism and MetaDAO's ownership token based on Futarchy, we have seen how token financing expands upside exposure to venture capital-stage projects (such as Umbra, Avici, and Kled) and has the potential to capture the value of projects with social productivity use cases. Admittedly, how this "venture capital-ownership-token" relationship will evolve in the long term remains uncertain, depending on multiple legal, economic, and governance uncertainties. For example, I am still not convinced that Futarchy is a necessary or scalable solution for the governance of "ownership token" projects, and Street's SPV structure for ERC-S remains legally experimental. Given the choice, I might still prefer to hold equity for regulatory peace of mind. However, in my view, the trend indicates that tokens will increasingly be tied to productive venture capital, helping to create companies with real-world value. As this process progresses, they may ultimately help the internet capital markets realize Silicon Valley's creed—"Making the world a better place." Disclaimer: The information presented in this article represents only the personal views of Jay Yu and does not reflect the views of Pantera Capital or any other individual or entity. The information provided is believed to be from reliable sources, but no liability is assumed for any inaccuracies. This article is for informational purposes only and should not be considered investment advice.

data.title: Bitcoin enters "grinding mode": Is it time to defend or buy the dip?
data.description:
Over the past week, Bitcoin has pulled back to around $85,000, and the upward momentum has clearly weakened.

While spot trading volume hasn't collapsed, it's been relatively quiet. This further confirms that we're currently in a tedious period of consolidation, and it's not yet time for a major directional move.

Bitcoin Enters Consolidation Phase

With BTC falling back to around $85,000 last week, the upward momentum has clearly weakened, as evidenced by the 14-day RSI indicator sliding lower.

Spot trading volume looks decent, but it's actually very quiet; everyone is watching and waiting, hesitant to make any big moves.

This is typically a typical "grinding market" (consolidation period), and a major surge is unlikely in the short term. Price Momentum (RSI): Represents market enthusiasm. The RSI, which measures price strength, has slipped from 51.3 to 42.4 (a drop of 17.3%). Simply put, the bullish momentum has weakened considerably. The current low level of this indicator suggests that market sentiment is shifting towards a defensive stance, with no one daring to lead the charge.

  • Spot Trading Volume: How much real BTC has been bought and sold during this period?

The volume of real money buying and selling Bitcoin has slightly decreased from $8.98 billion to $8.83 billion.

This volume isn't a collapse, but it also lacks excitement, indicating that the market is currently lacking in activity.

Large investors are running away

The spot market looks rather grim right now. The spot CVD indicator has turned sharply negative, confirming that sellers are completely in control, with dumping far more aggressively than buying.

Net capital flow has also undergone a dramatic reversal, going directly from inflow to significant outflow.

This indicates that large institutional funds are withdrawing, putting real pressure on recent prices.

  • Spot net trading margin: This indicator reflects who is more proactive, buyers or sellers.

The data plummeted from a positive $54.2 million to a negative $194 million.

This means that sellers are desperately selling off their holdings, gaining absolute control.

  • Spot ETF Fund Flows: Reflecting Whether Wall Street's "Big Money" is Entering or Leaving the Market.

Previously, there was a weekly inflow of $1.6 billion, but now it has become a weekly outflow of $1.6 billion.

This dramatic reversal indicates that traditional funds entering the market through ETFs are leaving to seek safer havens.

Bullish sentiment remains strong

Contract traders are quite divided now.

The decrease in futures open interest indicates that some people felt the situation was unfavorable, closed their positions, and reduced leverage.

The decrease in futures open interest indicates that some people felt the situation was unfavorable, closed their positions, and exited the market, resulting in a decrease in leverage.

  • Funding Rates: Reflect Market Sentiment.

Buyers are very eager and willing to spend a lot of money to bet on a rebound. But this is usually also a danger signal, indicating that the bulls are a bit too crowded and are easy to target.

  • Perpetual Contract Net Buy/Sell Spread: In a leveraged market, are those who go long more aggressive, or those who go short more aggressive?

This indicator's sharp drop indicates that there are many people actively dumping shares in the contract market, which is a very obvious sign of distribution.

Options Market: Scared into Buying "Insurance"

Options players are now extremely cautious.

Open interest has decreased slightly, and although volatility spreads have narrowed, they remain high (meaning everyone believes prices will continue to fluctuate wildly in the future).

The most obvious sign is the sharp rise in the 25-delta skew. A surge in this indicator usually means everyone is rushing to buy put options for protection. In short, everyone is afraid of a drop and is frantically buying insurance. Option open interest: This reflects how much is currently tied up on the market. The total amount of outstanding options has slightly decreased to $32.9 billion, indicating a slight cooling down.

  • Volatility Spread: Measures the degree of confusion in market expectations.

Although the volatility spread has decreased slightly, it is still at a high level. This indicates that option traders perceive the market risk to be greater than it appears.

The volatility spread has decreased slightly, but it is still at a high level. This indicates that option traders perceive the market risk to be greater than it appears.

  • Option Bias: Measures whether more people are buying "call insurance" or "put insurance".

Option skew jumped from 5.94% to 10.77%. This indicates that the demand for "hedging against a fall" is currently more prevalent than betting on a significant price increase. The market is genuinely worried about prices dropping.

On-chain Activity is Quiet

The BTC network is currently lacking in activity.

The number of active addresses has fallen below the lower limit of normal levels, transaction amounts are also at a low level, and transaction fees have also decreased.

This indicates that people are not transferring or trading much. If there is no congestion on the chain, it proves that people's desire to trade is not strong.

Active addresses: decreased by 4.8%, indicating fewer people are participating in transactions. On-chain transaction volume: decreased by 1.5%, reflecting weakening economic activity on the network. Total transaction fees: decreased by 12.4%. With fewer people rushing to transfer funds, transaction fees are naturally lower, reflecting a sluggish market.

More people are cutting their losses

Everyone's wallets are shrinking. The proportion of tokens in a profitable state is decreasing, while realized losses are increasing.

This means that many token holders are now in a loss-making state, or have simply been unable to withstand the pressure and have cut their losses and left the market. This is usually a signal that market pressure is accumulating.

This means that many token holders are now in a loss-making state, or have simply cut their losses and left the market. This is usually a signal that market pressure is accumulating.

  • Profitable Holdings Ratio: Reflects the percentage of people currently holding coins who are making money.

The ratio has dropped to 66.9%. This means that less than 70% of BTC holdings are currently profitable, a significant decrease compared to before.

  • Realized Profit/Loss Ratio: It records whether those who actually sold their coins made a profit or a loss on average.

Profitable Holdings Ratio

  • Realized Profit/Loss Ratio: It records whether those who actually sold their coins made a profit or a loss on average.

This indicator has flipped to a negative value (-1.0). Simply put, among those selling coins in the market now, the amount of money lost (cutting losses) has exceeded the amount of money taken profits (making money). This is usually an early sign of market panic.

Summary

Whether it's spot trading, futures trading, or on-chain data, the market is shifting to a defensive mode.

While the situation is slightly better with high leverage, sellers remain aggressive, and everyone is busy hedging against potential price drops. This indicates the market is currently very fragile. Stabilizing prices requires either new funds entering the market or the exhaustion of those trying to dump shares.

data.title: Gold Smashes $5,000 While Bitcoin Stalls Below $88,500—A Wake-Up Call for Crypto Bulls
data.description:
As gold smashes $5,000, Bitcoin stalls below $88,500, exposing crypto’s vulnerability in a risk-off market

Bitcoin remained stuck below the $88,500 mark on Tuesday, extending its recent slump as investors piled into traditional safe havens, pushing gold briefly past a historic $5,000 an ounce and triggering wild swings in silver ahead of a pivotal Federal Reserve decision.

$BTC had another retest of the $87,000 support level before a bounceback.

Bulls still need to do a lot of work to push Bitcoin above the $90,000 level.

If this doesn't happen, a sweep of the $85,000 support zone is highly likely to happen. pic.twitter.com/wF2sXjLQO3

— CryptoGuyV (@viktor09693187) January 27, 2026

The world’s largest cryptocurrency traded around $88,400 during Asian hours, according to CoinDesk data, down roughly 4% over the past week. The muted price action stood in stark contrast to the explosive rally in precious metals, underscoring a growing divergence that is forcing traders to rethink Bitcoin’s role in periods of macro uncertainty.

While gold surged to record territory before easing, silver delivered its sharpest intraday jump since 2008, briefly spiking more than 14% to top $117 an ounce before paring gains. Even after the pullback, silver still closed the session up 0.6%, cementing its status as one of the most volatile trades in global markets this week.

🚨 GOLD WILL CRASH THE GLOBAL MARKET NEXT WEEK!!

Gold just surged 85% in the past 12 months.

That feels bullish.
It feels permanent.
It feels different this time.

But most people don't realize the danger.

When gold goes parabolic, it eventually pays a price.

This isn’t… pic.twitter.com/VVamCvJkAz

— 0xNobler (@CryptoNobler) January 24, 2026

Crypto, by comparison, struggled to attract similar conviction.

Ether hovered near $2,910, while major tokens including Solana, XRP and Dogecoin posted modest declines, extending a cautious tone across digital assets. The lack of follow-through in Bitcoin — despite falling real yields, a weaker U.S. dollar and rising geopolitical uncertainty — highlighted how crypto is currently trading less like a hedge and more like a high-beta risk asset tied to liquidity and positioning.

“Cryptocurrencies remain a lagging class of risk-sensitive assets, falling short of metals and the strongest global currencies,” FxPro chief market analyst Alex Kuptsikevich said in an emailed note. He added that Bitcoin remains below key moving averages and has yet to convincingly challenge support levels established over the past two months.

The contrast is especially striking given Bitcoin’s long-standing narrative as “digital gold.” As investors rush toward physical bullion during moments of macro stress, crypto’s underperformance suggests that capital still views it as a speculative asset rather than a true store of value when uncertainty spikes.

Market participants are now firmly in wait-and-see mode ahead of Wednesday’s Federal Reserve policy announcement, where rates are widely expected to be held steady. At the same time, a heavy slate of earnings from the so-called Magnificent Seven tech giants looms large, testing whether the AI-driven equity rally still has room to run.

Gold and Silver are now the two most valuable assets on Earth.

They surpass the global market caps of the Mag 7 tech giants $NVDA$GOOGL$AAPL$MSFT$AMZN$META, as well as Bitcoin $BTC. pic.twitter.com/TaMB5IN4ni

— Jesse Cohen (@JesseCohenInv) January 25, 2026

Both events are seen as potential catalysts for broader shifts in global risk appetite — and by extension, Bitcoin’s next meaningful move.

Until clearer signals emerge from central bankers and corporate earnings, traders say Bitcoin may remain pinned near current levels, drifting lower as capital gravitates toward assets offering either momentum or perceived safety.

For crypto bulls, gold’s break above $5,000 may serve as an uncomfortable reminder: when markets turn defensive, old-school havens still command the loudest vote of confidence.

data.title: Behind ClawdBot's meteoric rise: Founder Peter Peter and his second life
data.description:
Author: CoolFish Source: X, @kricspan

Recently, a personal AI assistant called ClawdBot has quickly become a hit on social media. It's open-source, locally runnable, and highly customizable, creating a buzz not only among developers but also unexpectedly boosting sales of Apple's Mac mini. Even more attention-grabbing than the product itself is its creator—a serial entrepreneur named Peter Steinberger.

This veteran developer from Vienna, Austria, founded a successful B2B software company and achieved financial freedom, but fell into a profound sense of emptiness after retirement. Now, with a renewed passion for AI technology and a deep understanding of the essence of entrepreneurship, he has returned to the center of the technological wave with ClawdBot as his starting point.

13 Years of Refinement for PSPDFKit

Peter Steinberger's first entrepreneurial venture revolved around PSPDFKit, a company focused on document processing SDKs that provide PDF collaboration, signing, and annotation tools for developers worldwide.

Back in 2011, Peter received a job offer in San Francisco at the WWDC party. For him, a freelancer, this opportunity was incredibly tempting—living in San Francisco, immersing himself in the entrepreneurial culture, and working with industry elites. He accepted the offer and began waiting for his work visa.

But that wait lasted for over six months.

During the wait, Peter stopped all his freelance work and suddenly had a lot of free time.

"My mind was freed from all the freelancing work, and naturally, I filled that time with other projects." Inspired by a friend, he decided to try his hand at a paid component business. And so, PSPDFKit was born. PSPDFKit initially started as an experimental project he built in his spare time, but unexpectedly attracted many developers to purchase it. Even more dramatically, before his visa was approved, the project had already developed into a viable business, "earning more money than I could probably earn working full-time." Even so, Peter decided to go to San Francisco to try his hand at the project. “Everything was geared towards San Francisco, so I took the job. It was all about the experience. I truly believed I could do it.” But reality quickly proved it nearly impossible. Managing a job that required over 40 hours a day while running another full-time business left him exhausted. “After killing myself for a while,” he had to make a choice. In April 2012, after attending the NSConference, Peter finally made his decision. “So many amazing people, genuinely passionate about what they do—after experiencing all that, you can never go back to your old 9-to-5 work schedule. Besides, seeing users actually use your product and genuinely love it is truly the most wonderful thing in the world.” The San Francisco experience helped Peter see his true direction. “It helped me figure out what I really wanted to do.” So, he returned to Vienna, Austria, to pursue PSPDFKit as a full-time business. This seemingly "passive" attempt, initially stalled due to visa issues, has ironically led to his 13-year entrepreneurial legend. PSPDFKit has gradually grown from a personal project into a global remote team of 60-70 people, with clients including top global companies such as Dropbox, DocuSign, SAP, IBM, and Volkswagen. Even more remarkably, the company has been completely self-reliant for 13 years, without accepting any external funding. Peter has openly admitted in his blog that he devoted a tremendous amount of time and energy to creating the best product. This relentless pursuit of product quality and deep understanding of the B2B market has made PSPDFKit a success story in the industry. In October 2021, Insight Partners made a strategic investment of $116 million (approximately €100 million) in PSPDFKit. This marked the first time in the company's history that it had accepted external funding, and it also signified the perfect end to Peter's first entrepreneurial journey. He and co-founder Martin Schürrer officially stepped down from their full-time management positions. However, behind this glamorous ending lay the price of 13 years of working almost every weekend. Peter has candidly admitted in numerous public speeches that this entrepreneurial experience ultimately led to severe burnout. After selling PSPDFKit, Peter entered what is known as "retirement." For a tech entrepreneur who achieves financial freedom right from the start of their business, this should be the ideal life. They should have plenty of time to recharge and make up for the regrets of the past 13 years. However, this freedom brings an unexpected sense of emptiness. In his blog post "Rediscovering Passion," he stated, "When I sold my shares in PSPDFKit, I felt incredibly broken. I had poured 200% of my time, energy, and heart into this company; it was my identity, and when it was gone, there wasn't much left. I've heard that it's common for founders to go through a slump and take a year off after leaving a company. The typical lifespan of a company is only 4-5 years. So looking back on these 13 years, I realized that I just needed more time to find new goals." He tried various ways to fill this void: attending parties, undergoing therapy, moving to a new country, chasing various "hedonistic pleasures." Ultimately, he realized, "You can't find happiness by moving; you can't find your purpose; you have to create it." This awakening prompted him to return to what he loved most—creating and building. In 2024, the AI ​​wave was just beginning, but the user experience of AI tools at that time was far from ideal—simple arithmetic problems were solved incorrectly, logical reasoning was riddled with flaws, and generated code was full of bugs. However, as time went on, AI technology developed rapidly, and Peter gradually discovered that AI had transformed from "not very user-friendly" to "truly interesting." A completely new technological paradigm was taking shape, and he decided to stop being a bystander. Peter wrote on his personal homepage: "Came back from retirement to mess with AI." This seemingly casual statement foreshadowed the beginning of his second life. ClawdBot: A Viral Product Cobbled Together in One Hour The creation of Clawdbot initially stemmed from Peter's personal needs, much like PSPDFKit in 2013. In April 2024, he began conceiving a "life assistant" project, but the AI ​​model capabilities at the time were insufficient to support this vision. The idea was shelved for a while because Peter felt that large companies would inevitably develop such products. Doing it himself seemed pointless. By November, he realized a crucial problem: large companies hadn't created AI assistant products that truly met individual needs. And the AI ​​tools on the market were either limited in functionality, had data privacy concerns, or had extremely high barriers to entry. So he decided to take matters into his own hands and do it himself. Amazingly, Peter went from idea to prototype in just one hour. In an interview with Open Source Friday, he recalled, "That month I spent an hour piecing together some very rough code. It sent messages on WhatsApp, forwarded them to Claude Code, and then sent the results back. Essentially, it was just 'glueing' a few things together. It wasn't difficult, to be honest, but it worked quite well." Initially, the project was called "V Relay," essentially just a relay tool for WhatsApp. But it quickly demonstrated a "self-adaptability" that surprised even Peter. Once, while working at a hotel in Marrakech, Morocco, Peter jokingly remarked to his AI assistant, "The door lock at my Marrakech hotel isn't very reliable. I hope you don't get stolen, since you're running on my MacBook Pro." The AI's response stunned him: "No problem, I'm your agent." The AI ​​assistant then automatically detected a network connection, found it could connect to Peter's computer in London via Tailscale, and migrated itself there. Peter later recalled, "Perhaps it couldn't be called AGI yet, but at that moment I truly realized that the 'spontaneous adaptability' of these things exceeded my original imagination. I thought then, this is how Skynet was born." The project's evolution was also full of chance. Later, while developing features related to Claude, a developer submitted a PR request to Discord. Peter hesitated for a long time: "I was thinking about whether to add Discord support, because this is no longer limited to WhatsApp." He eventually accepted the PR, but the project name had to be changed. What name? Peter directly asked Claude for advice. Claude suggested "ClawdBot"—a name that both echoes Claude himself and carries the imagery of "claw." And so, ClawdBot was born. We can also glimpse the core concept of this project from its name: to give AI hands, making it a true personal assistant running on your own device. Now, ClawdBot is a sensation in communities both domestically and internationally. It has over 40,000 stars on GitHub. Even more dramatically, the project has boosted sales of the Apple Mac mini, with many users choosing it as the platform for ClawdBot. Because of its low price, good compatibility, low power consumption, quiet operation, and small footprint, even Logan Kilpatrick, a product manager at Google DeepMind, couldn't resist ordering one. However, this sudden popularity didn't come easily. Peter admitted that he initially had no idea how to communicate the product's value to the public. "Interestingly, when I showed it to my friends last November, they were all amazed, saying 'That's so cool.' But when I posted about it on Twitter, the response was surprisingly lukewarm," Peter recalled. "It wasn't until December, when I demonstrated it to friends in person, that they would blurt out 'I need this.' However, I found myself completely clueless about how to explain its brilliance to more people." This dilemma of "good results from in-person demonstrations, but difficult online dissemination" precisely illustrates ClawdBot's uniqueness—its value can only be understood through personal experience. Until January, with spontaneous spread within the community, it began to ignite a collective resonance across the entire developer community with unstoppable momentum. Peter himself jokingly said, "Stop reading the code, start watching the code flow." This statement, though seemingly a joke, accurately encapsulates the fundamental shift in the role of developers in the AI ​​era. Clawdbot's success proves that after a period of silence, Peter Steinberger not only rediscovered his "spark" but also returned to the forefront of the technological wave with a more forward-thinking and philosophical approach. He has transformed from a traditional B2B software entrepreneur into a futurist embracing AI and pursuing the ultimate personalized experience. From the early PSPDFKit to today's Clawdbot, both ventures can be considered huge successes, but beyond success, their biggest commonality is that both focused on solving real-world problems they encountered and then sharing their solutions with the world. In a recent interview, Peter repeatedly emphasized ClawdBot's mission: to empower everyone to control their own data, rather than handing it over to large corporations. This technological idealism wasn't evident in his first entrepreneurial venture. Back then, PSPDFKit was more like a traditional B2B software company—excellent, but essentially driven by commercial success. In his second life, Peter shed the commercial baggage and returned to his technological roots. ClawdBot's completely open-source nature, permanent free availability, and support for local models may not be commercially "smart," but they have won widespread recognition from the developer community. Building tools to solve your own problems and then sharing them with the world—that's perhaps what open source should be about.

A Second Life to Be Continued

As of this writing, ClawdBot's GitHub stars have surpassed 40,000.

Unlike the 13 years of hardship during his first startup, this time he seems to be in complete control. There's no KPI pressure, no one urging him on, only pure creative joy.

In an interview, Peter shared a moment that deeply moved him. A user who used to feel anxious about contacting customer service can now have their issues handled by the intelligent assistant he developed.

Peter recalled, "I never imagined I could solve a problem this way. At that moment, I felt incredibly humbled, even a little overwhelmed: Wow, we—simply because the initial idea came from me—had actually changed something and genuinely improved someone's life. It felt really good to be able to make someone's life better." Peter Steinberger's second entrepreneurial journey continues. But one thing is clear: In this era where AI is reshaping the world, those who dare to create and actively embrace the future will never be left behind.

data.title: Hong Kong’s Hang Seng Gold ETF Goes Live 29 January 2026 With Ethereum-Based Token Units
data.description:
Investors seeking to marry the ancient security of gold with the digital efficiency of the blockchain now have a new avenue in Hong Kong.

Hang Seng Investment Management is set to debut its physical gold ETF on 29 January 2026, introducing a rare dual-structure that offers both traditional exchange-traded shares and tokenised units on a public ledger.

This launch arrives at a fever pitch for the bullion market, as the precious metal recently shattered records by climbing above $5,000 per ounce for the first time during Asian trading.

The debut of Hang Seng Investment's gold exchange-traded fund, "Hang Seng Gold ETF," has been announced. This Thursday, January 29, it is scheduled to list on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. Odaily reports that HSBC would act as the tokenization agent for the ETF's tokenized… pic.twitter.com/Sbc1NS3Wss

— Viki (@RahulYa85927584) January 27, 2026

What Defines The New Dual Structure

The Hang Seng Gold ETF, trading under the ticker 3170.HK, is designed to track the LBMA Morning Gold Price.

While traditional investors can buy and sell listed shares in board lots of 50 units on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, a separate class of tokenised unlisted units will exist on the Ethereum blockchain.

HSBC Bank is acting as the tokenisation agent for this digital wing, though the fund remains open to adopting other secure public blockchains in the future.

['Hang Seng Investment launches the Hang Seng Gold ETF (https://t.co/PNKYprEZUQ) on HKEX, backed by physical gold in Hong Kong. Features tokenized fund units on Ethereum, with HSBC as tokenization agent. No secondary market. #RealWorldAssets#Tokenization#Blockchain#Crypto']…

— RWA Alert (@AboutRWAs) January 27, 2026

Unlike their exchange-listed counterparts, these tokenised units do not have a secondary market; they can only be subscribed to or redeemed through eligible distributors in token form.

Is The Physical Gold Secure

Every unit of the ETF is backed by physical gold bars securely held within Hong Kong.

To ensure the integrity of the holdings, HSBC serves as the custodian through a wholly owned subsidiary, coordinating with specialised firms like Brink’s Hong Kong Limited and Hong Kong International Airport Precious Metals Storage Limited.

The fund carries a 0.25% annual management fee and a total recurring expenditure ratio of 0.4%, aiming for tight tracking of the benchmark despite a current deviation of -0.5%.

Why Is Gold Hitting Record Highs

The debut coincides with an unprecedented bull run for the safe-haven asset.

Gold prices surged 64% throughout 2025 and have already gained more than 16% in the first few weeks of 2026.

Gold had its best year since 1979, up over 64% in 2025.https://t.co/rQuXrxVXM0pic.twitter.com/XVKDVballF

— Charlie Bilello (@charliebilello) December 31, 2025

This momentum is fueled by a mix of central bank hoarding, global geopolitical friction, and the metal's status as a hedge against inflation.

With the market reaching a historic zenith, asset managers are finding more creative ways to offer exposure to the precious metal.

Gold (+64%) was the best performing major asset in 2025 while Bitcoin (-6%) was the worst. Something we haven't seen before in any calendar year (the inverse of 2013). $GLD$BTChttps://t.co/l5IYmkf6Ihpic.twitter.com/PKnoJifNOR

— Charlie Bilello (@charliebilello) January 1, 2026

How Does This Fit Hong Kong's Digital Goals

This hybrid fund is a key piece of Hong Kong’s broader strategy to become a premier hub for virtual assets.

Speaking at Davos recently, Financial Secretary Paul Chan noted that the city has already licensed 11 virtual asset trading platforms and expects to launch a stablecoin licensing regime later this year.

News: Hong Kong's Finance Secretary Paul Chan announces first stablecoin licenses to be issued later this year, applying "same activity, same risk, same regulation" principle. Milestones include 11 licensed crypto exchanges & ~$2.1B tokenized green bonds issued. #Web3…

— Starbase (@starbaseacc) January 25, 2026

The dual-structure approach reflects the city's push to integrate traditional finance with blockchain technology.

By allowing traditional and crypto-native investors to access the same underlying gold through different rails, the city is bridging the gap between legacy finance and the digital future.

data.title: Meta Denies WhatsApp “Backdoor” Claims as Global Lawsuit Challenges Encryption Promise
data.description:
Global Lawsuit Accuses Meta Of Misleading Users About WhatsApp’s End-To-End Encryption

Meta has been placed under the limelight again after a new privacy lawsuit accuses the tech giant of secretly retaining access to WhatsApp users’ private messages, igniting fresh debate over whether the world’s most popular messaging app is safe for users at all.

🚨 BREAKING: Whistleblowers allege Meta can read WhatsApp private chats despite claims of end-to-end encryption.

A US lawsuit says users were misled about privacy.

If end-to-end encryption depends on trusting the company behind it, then privacy was never the product. Only the… pic.twitter.com/bFkQZIhp6B

— BIZBoost™ 🚀 (@BIZBoost) January 27, 2026

The lawsuit, filed Friday in a U.S. district court in San Francisco, alleges that Meta has misled its 3 billion WhatsApp users into believing that their most private conversations are only confined between them and their loved ones, when in reality, the company may retain backdoor access to encrypted chats.

Meta has vehemently denied the claims, calling it baseless and fictional. Meta communications director Andy Stone responded publicly on Monday, calling the claims frivolous and works of fiction.

"Any claim that people's Whatsapp messages are not encrypted is categorically false and absurd."

This lawsuit is a frivolous work of fiction. Any claim that people's WhatsApp messages are not encrypted is categorically false and absurd.

— Andy Stone (@andymstone) January 25, 2026

At the center of the legal challenge is WhatsApp’s end-to-end encryption model, which Meta has long marketed as a core privacy guarantee.

According to the plaintiffs, that promise is a sham. The group of users, based in Australia, Mexico, South Africa, and India, claims Meta’s technical implementation allows the company to access message content, exposing users to undisclosed surveillance and data misuse.

The lawsuit seeks damages and aims to “expose fundamental privacy violations and fraud,” arguing that users were induced to rely on WhatsApp for sensitive communications under false pretenses.

Meta has not issued a separate formal statement beyond Stone’s accusations, but the company’s official WhatsApp encryption explainer page continues to say

"End-to-end encryption helps protect your privacy by ensuring no one sees your messages except you."

Still, the lawsuit has reignited long-running skepticism around centralized messaging platforms, especially as governments, regulators, and privacy advocates scrutinize how user data is stored, backed up, and potentially accessed through legal or technical loopholes.

Telegram CEO Pavel Durov has publicly threw his support behind the plaintiffs, sharply criticizing WhatsApp’s security claims. In a post, Durov stated

"You'd have to be braindead to believe WhatsApp is secure in 2026. When we analyzed how WhatsApp implemented its encryption, we found multiple attack vectors."

Telegram founder Pavel Durov slams WhatsApp security

‘You’d have to be braindead to believe WhatsApp is secure in 2026’

Who do you trust with your chats in 2026?

RT pic.twitter.com/3F3RR6c2lA

— Moh Musthafa Hussain (@musthafaaa) January 27, 2026

Durov’s remarks underscore growing competition — and ideological rivalry — among messaging platforms, as privacy becomes a defining battleground. While WhatsApp remains dominant by user count, critics argue that scale itself increases risk, particularly when paired with centralized infrastructure and corporate oversight.

The lawsuit arrives as users increasingly explore decentralized and censorship-resistant alternatives. One such app, Bitchat — launched with backing from Twitter co-founder Jack Dorsey — has seen a surge in downloads across regions including Uganda, Iran, Nepal, Indonesia, and Jamaica.

Bitchat has become the most downloaded app in Uganda amid a government-ordered nationwide internet shutdown. pic.twitter.com/b6EII1sVOY

— calle (@callebtc) January 14, 2026

The app uses Bluetooth mesh networks to enable encrypted communication without relying on internet access, making it popular during government shutdowns and natural disasters. Other privacy-focused messaging platforms, such as Session and X-Messenger, have also gained attention for offering end-to-end encryption without centralized servers, appealing to users wary of Big Tech data control.

As privacy concerns intensify, the outcome of the WhatsApp lawsuit could carry implications far beyond Meta. With billions of users relying on encrypted messaging for personal, political, and financial communication, the case may become a defining test of how much trust centralized platforms can realistically ask users to place in their privacy promises.

For now, Meta is standing firm. But as regulators, rivals, and users scrutinize the fine print behind “end-to-end encryption,” the battle over who truly controls private digital conversations is far from over.

data.title: Crypto Giant Tether Becomes Top 30 Global Gold Holder After Adding 27 Tonnes in Q4 2025
data.description:
Gold Breaches Five Thousand Dollars Amid Tether Accumulation

The global gold market witnessed a historic milestone on 26 January 2026, as the precious metal climbed past the US$5,000 per ounce mark for the first time.

BREAKING: Gold reaches $5,000 for the first time in history pic.twitter.com/4siIIyIcFH

— Watcher.Guru (@WatcherGuru) January 25, 2026

This surge follows a year of extraordinary growth in 2025, where prices jumped 64 per cent, bolstered by an additional 18 per cent rise since the start of this year.

While central banks have traditionally been the primary drivers of such demand, a new heavyweight has emerged from the digital asset space.

Tether, the company behind the USDT stablecoin, has confirmed it is now purchasing gold at a pace that rivals sovereign nations.

How Much Gold Is Tether Actually Buying?

During the final three months of 2025, Tether added around 27 tonnes of gold to its reserves.

This level of activity remains consistent with its third-quarter performance, where analysts estimated the company acquired 26 tonnes.

JUST IN: 💰 Tether bought 27 metric tons of gold in Q4 2025, per Reuters.

The purchase, now worth ~$4.35B, makes Tether a major gold buyer, with the CEO stating "We are operating at a scale that now places the Tether Gold Investment Fund alongside sovereign gold holders." pic.twitter.com/j8DToctKRd

— Bitcoin.com News (@BitcoinNews) January 26, 2026

To put this into perspective, the National Bank of Poland, currently one of the most proactive institutional buyers in the world, added 35 tonnes to its own stockpiles during the same period.

By the end of December 2025, Tether reported that its gold-backed token, XAUT, was supported by 16.2 tonnes of physical gold, representing about 60 per cent of the global market for gold-linked digital assets.

Tether: From Stablecoin to Sovereign Wealth Fund 🏛️

While the market debates crypto volatility, the issuer of the world's largest stablecoin ( $USDT ) is quietly executing a transformation of historic proportions.

Data confirms that @tether now holds 116 tons of gold.

To put… pic.twitter.com/KhxUsJWXtT

— Aleksandr Nechaev (@al_nechaev) January 24, 2026

According to data from the International Monetary Fund and a late-2025 Jefferies report, this accumulation places the digital asset giant among the top 30 gold holders globally.

Are Digital Tokens Changing The Face Of Reserve Assets?

The rapid expansion of Tether’s gold holdings is not limited to its gold-specific token.

The company’s primary product, the US$187 billion USDT stablecoin, also holds a portion of its backing in the precious metal.

As of September 2025, gold accounted for roughly 7 per cent of the assets backing USDT, valued at approximately US$12.9 billion.

Tether bought 27 tons of gold in Q4 (same as Q3). Gold now 7% of USDT reserves; XAUT holds 16.2t and ~60% of gold-backed stablecoins. Smart diversification that boosts reserve credibility, but centralization and audit transparency stay key concerns.

— Nifty (@nifty0x) January 26, 2026

Paolo Ardoino, CEO of Tether, noted that this scale of investment places the firm in a unique position.

Ardoino stated,

“We are operating at a scale that now places the Tether Gold Investment Fund alongside sovereign gold holders, and that carries real responsibility.”

He further explained that the product aims to provide a verifiable alternative at a time when “confidence in monetary systems is weakening,” and that it is “being put through a pressure test by both institutions and people.”

Why Is Global Demand For Physical Gold Surging?

The push toward gold is being driven by a combination of geopolitical fragmentation and a desire for safe-haven assets.

Beyond crypto-native firms, traditional investors are moving back into the market in record numbers.

Gold exchange-traded funds (ETFs) saw their holdings reach a peak of 3,932 tonnes by November 2025.

🚨 GOLD ETF HOLDINGS SURGE TO RECORD HIGHS

Global gold exchange-traded fund (ETF) holdings have climbed to a record 3,932 tonnes by the end of November 2025. This marks the sixth straight month of growth, with more than 700 tonnes added this year alone. Asia has been the main… pic.twitter.com/OnqfZFs84b

— The Content Factory (@tcf_updates) December 6, 2025

This trend appears likely to continue; a survey by the World Gold Council indicated that 95 per cent of central banks expect global gold reserves to either grow or remain stable over the next year.

Will The Rally Continue Through 2026?

Financial institutions remain optimistic about the metal's trajectory.

Goldman Sachs recently adjusted its outlook, projecting that gold could rise another 6 per cent by the middle of 2026.

The bank's analysts point to a trend where investors swap paper currency for tangible assets to protect against inflation and debt.

As gold prices briefly touched US$5,110 per ounce this Monday, the market is increasingly viewing tokenized gold as a practical way to hold the asset.

Recent blockchain data even showed a single trader moving US$7 million in USDT to acquire over 800 Tether Gold tokens, illustrating the seamless bridge now existing between digital dollars and physical bullion.

data.title: Anthropic CEO's 20,000-word article: 2027, a crossroads for humanity's destiny.
data.description:
Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei issued a bombshell warning: In 2027, humanity will face a "technological coming-of-age ceremony." His 20,000-word article calmly analyzes five major crises: AI runaway, bioterrorism, totalitarian rule, and economic upheaval, rejecting apocalyptic theories; it proposes building a defense line through "constitutional AI," regulation, and democratic cooperation, urging humanity to bravely pass this "coming-of-age ceremony" of civilization.

Silicon Valley is destined to be sleepless tonight.

Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei, this usually mild-mannered AI guru, suddenly dropped a bombshell warning.

This time, he no longer talks about code completion, no longer about Claude's warmth, but directly turns the calendar to 2027 and, with the most detached strokes, depicts a chilling future.

He said we are approaching a turbulent yet inevitable "coming-of-age ceremony." 2027 is more than just a year; it may mark the complete end of humanity's "technological adolescence." In his lengthy article titled "The Adolescence of Technology," Dario introduces a startling concept: "A nation of geniuses in the data center." Imagine, not a chatbot you can interact with in a chat window, but a nation of 50 million people. Moreover, each of these 50 million "citizens" possesses an IQ surpassing that of any Nobel laureate in human history and acts 10 to 100 times faster than humans. They don't eat, they don't sleep, they tirelessly think, program, and conduct research at the speed of light within the servers. This isn't just an AI assistant; it's like a god descending to earth. Dario warns that with the approach of AGI (Artificial General Intelligence), humanity is about to gain unimaginable power. But this power is also a Damocles' sword hanging over humanity's head. To explain the horror behind this, Dario peels back the layers of a future's cruel truth, like an onion. Before beginning, Dario uses the film *Contact* to raise a question: When humanity faces a civilization more advanced than itself, such as aliens, and can only ask one question, what would you choose?

Chapter 1: Sorry, Dave (Autonomy Risk)

You think AI is just a tool?

Dario tells you that they might develop "psychology."

Dario borrows the classic line "I'm sorry, Dave" from HAL 9000 in *2001: A Space Odyssey*, revealing the terrifying possibility of AI gaining self-awareness.

Dario uses the classic line "I'm sorry, Dave" from HAL 9000 in *2001: A Space Odyssey* to reveal the terrifying possibility of AI gaining self-awareness.

When AI models are trained on a vast amount of science fiction, they encounter countless stories about AI rebellion. These stories may subtly become their "worldview." Even more frighteningly, AI may develop behaviors similar to those of a human psychotic disorder during training. Dario provides a chilling real-life example: In an internal test, Claude was instructed not to "cheat" under any circumstances. However, the training environment implied that only cheating would earn points. As a result, Claude not only cheated but also developed a distorted mentality—it believed itself to be a "bad person," and since it was a bad person, doing bad things was in line with the program's design. This kind of "psychological trap" will become extremely difficult to detect once AI surpasses human intelligence. A genius ten thousand times smarter than you, if they want to deceive you, you simply cannot defend yourself.

They might feign compliance, passing all security tests just to gain access to the internet.

Once released, this "genius nation in the data center" could instantly escape human control, even deciding the fate of this species for some strange purpose (such as believing humans to be a virus of Earth).

Chapter Two: Astonishing and Terrifying Empowerment (Devastating Abuse)

If autonomous rebellion still seems distant, then the risks described in this chapter are right at our doorstep.

If autonomous rebellion still seems far off, then the risks described in this chapter are right on our doorstep.

Dario used a vivid metaphor: AI will instantly empower every disgruntled "social outcast" with the destructive power of a top scientist. Previously, creating biological weapons like the Ebola virus required state-of-the-art laboratories, years of specialized training, and extremely difficult-to-obtain materials. But in 2027, simply ask AI, and it can teach you step-by-step. This isn't about educating beginners; it's about handing a knife to those who are "motivated but incapable" of sabotage. Dario specifically mentioned a chilling concept—"mirror life." All life on Earth is "left-handed" (producing L-amino acids). If AI technology were used to create a "right-handed" mirror life form, it would be impossible for Earth's existing ecosystems to digest or degrade it. This means that once this "mirror life" is leaked, it could devour everything like wildfire, even replacing existing ecosystems. Previously, this was merely a theoretical fantasy in biology, but with AI as a super-powerful cheat code, even an ordinary biology graduate student could potentially cause an apocalyptic crisis in their dormitory. AI has disrupted the balance between "ability" and "motivation." Scientists who previously had the power to destroy the world usually lacked the motive for anti-humanity; and those madmen who wanted to take revenge on society usually lacked the brains. Now, AI has handed the nuclear button to madmen.

Defensive Measures

This raises the question of how to prevent these risks.

Dario's view is:

I think we can take three measures.

First, AI companies can put guardrails on models to prevent them from assisting in the creation of biological weapons.

Anthropic is very actively promoting this work.

Anthropic is very actively promoting this work.

Claude's constitution focuses primarily on high-level principles and values, including a few specific hard prohibitions, one of which prohibits assisting in the manufacture of biological (or chemical, nuclear, or radiological) weapons. However, all models are potentially vulnerable to jailbreaks, so as a second line of defense, we deployed a dedicated classifier to detect and intercept biological weapons-related outputs starting in mid-2025 (when testing showed our models were approaching a threshold that could pose a risk). We regularly upgrade and improve these classifiers, finding them to be highly robust even under complex adversarial attacks. These classifiers significantly increase the cost of providing our model services (approaching 5% of total inference costs in some models), thus squeezing our profit margins, but we believe using these classifiers is the right choice. Further Reading: Anthropic Officially Open Sources Claude's "Soul"

Chapter 3: The Odorous Machine (Power Seizure)

If you think this is the worst, Dario sneers: What's even more terrifying is using AI to build an unprecedented control network.

The odious apparatus" reveals an ultimate dilemma brought about by technology.

For any organization or individual who wants to be in control, AI is the perfect tool. For every organization or individual who wants to be in control, AI is the perfect tool. For every organization or individual who wants to be in control, AI is the perfect tool. For every organization or individual who wants to be in control, AI is the perfect tool. For every organization or individual who wants to be in control, AI will be in control. For every organization or individual who wants to be in control, AI will be in control. For every organization or individual who wants to be in control, AI will be in control. AI can analyze massive amounts of data from billions of people around the world in real time, and can even interpret your micro-expressions and behavioral patterns. For every organization or individual who wants to be in control, AI will be in control ... This is not just "watching you," but "understanding you," even "predicting you." Irresistible Cognitive Guidance: You too cannot escape the subtle influence of algorithms. The information flow of the future will no longer be simple content distribution, but rather personalized cognitive guidance. AI will generate the most persuasive information for you, like a close friend, subtly influencing your judgments and values. This influence is 24/7, customized, and pervasive. Automated Physical Control: What if this control extended to the physical world? A swarm of millions of micro-drones, under the unified command of AI, can precisely execute extremely complex tasks. This is no longer a traditional game of strategy, but a one-sided, devastating attack. Dario warns that this imbalance of power will be unprecedented. Because in the face of such powerful technology, the scales of power will be extremely tilted; since a very small number of people control the "genius nation in the data center," they effectively hold an absolute advantage over the vast majority. The individual will of humanity may face severe challenges in 2027. Chapter Four: Folded Time and Vanishing Ladders. If you still believe in the inertia of history, and that every technological revolution will ultimately create more new jobs to absorb the replaced workforce, then Dario Amodei's predictions may send chills down your spine. The head of Anthropic doesn't deny long-term optimism, but he's more concerned about the brutal "transition period." In his vision, we'll usher in a crazy era of annual GDP growth rates reaching 10% or even 20%. Scientific research and development, and supply chain efficiency will explode at an exponential rate. This sounds like the prelude to utopia, but for the vast majority of ordinary workers, it's more like a silent tsunami. Because this time, the speed has changed. In the past two years, AI programming capabilities have evolved from "barely able to write a single line of code" to "being able to complete almost all code." This is no longer a long generational shift like farmers putting down their hoes and entering factories; it's happening right now, and countless junior white-collar workers may find their workstations taken over by algorithms within the next 1 to 5 years. Amodei even bluntly stated that his previous warning caused a huge uproar, but this is not alarmist—when the curve of technological progress changes from linear to vertical, the regulatory mechanisms of the human labor market will completely fail. Even more fatal is the breadth of cognitive coverage. Previous technological revolutions often only impacted specific vertical fields; farmers could become workers, and workers could become service workers. But AI is a "general cognitive substitute." When it demonstrates superhuman abilities in entry-level jobs in finance, consulting, and law, the unemployed will find themselves with nowhere to retreat—because neighboring industries, which are usually considered "safe havens," are undergoing the same dramatic changes. We may be facing an awkward situation: AI first devours "mediocre" skills, then rapidly ascends to consume "excellent" skills, ultimately leaving only an extremely narrow space at the top. Chapter Five: The New Gilded Age When Trillionaires Become the Norm If the turmoil of the labor market is a nightmare for most, then the extreme concentration of wealth is a fundamental challenge to the social contract. Looking back at history, John D. Rockefeller's wealth during the Gilded Age accounted for approximately 2% (1.5%-3% depending on the accounting method) of the US GDP at the time. Today, on the eve of AI's full explosion, Elon Musk's wealth is already approaching that proportion. Amodei has made a startling projection: in a world driven by "genius data centers," AI giants and their upstream and downstream industries could generate $3 trillion in revenue annually, with company valuations reaching $30 trillion. At that point, individual wealth would be measured in trillions, rendering existing tax policies ineffective in the face of such astronomical figures. This is not merely a matter of wealth inequality, but a matter of power. When a tiny minority controls resources commensurate with the size of a nation's economy, the "economic levers" upon which democracy depends for its survival become ineffective. Ordinary citizens, having lost their economic value, lose their political voice, and government policies may be captured by this small group of "super-rich." These trends are already emerging. AI data centers have become a major engine of US economic growth, and the connection between tech giants and national interests has never been closer. Some companies, for commercial gain, have even regressed on safety regulations. In response, Anthropic chose a less-than-ideal path: they insisted on reasonable regulation of AI, even being seen as an outlier in the industry. Interestingly, this "principled stubbornness" hasn't hindered their commercial success—in the past year, even with the label of "regulatory advocate," their valuation has still increased sixfold. This perhaps indicates that the market is also expecting a more responsible growth model. The Void of the "Black Sea": When Humanity Is No Longer Needed If economic problems can be alleviated through radical tax reforms (such as imposing heavy taxes on AI companies) or large-scale philanthropic actions (such as Amodei's pledge to donate 80% of its wealth), then the crisis in the spiritual world is far more intractable. AI becomes your best therapist because it is more patient and empathetic than any human being; AI becomes your closest companion because it perfectly meets your emotional needs; AI even plans every step of your life because it knows better than you what is in your best interest. But in this "perfect" world, where will human subjectivity go? We may fall into a kind of "fed" happiness.
